diagrams.description: This mind map outlines the fundamental structure of Game Theory. From the central root, branches cover Key Elements (players, strategies, payoffs, information), Game Types (cooperative, non-cooperative, zero-sum, simultaneous, sequential, perfect/imperfect info), Solution Concepts (dominant strategy, Nash equilibrium, subgame perfect, Bayesian Nash, evolutionary stable), and Applications (economics—oligopoly, auctions; political science—voting, conflict; biology—evolution; computer science—AI, networks). It provides a comprehensive map of the theory’s components.
